definition: Gold miner credit is in a structurally favorable position due to central bank buying regime shift (1,000+ tonnes/year post-2022). AISC coverage ratio at 1.5-1.7x at current prices is robust. Key uncertainty is whether central bank demand is durable or front-loaded diversification that plateaus.
evidence: AISC coverage ratio 1.5-1.7x at current gold prices
Central bank buying at 1,000+ tonnes/year post-2022
Single agent origination with no corroboration
open_questions: Will central bank gold buying plateau as diversification objectives are met?
How does the gold price floor translate to specific credit metric thresholds?
Is the buying regime structural or could it reverse with geopolitical shifts?
